190.reason: [Unknown characters should not generate a "HELL" token; instead, they should trigger an error or be skipped, but the problem states only error 'a' exists, so this may incorrectly mask other lexical errors]

113.reason: [Appending a dummy "HELL" token with Null type on lexical error is incorrect; the lexer should not emit any token for invalid single '&' and must only record the error. Emitting a Null token may interfere with later stages or output logic.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Appending a dummy "HELL" token with Null type on lexical error is incorrect; the lexer should not emit any token for invalid single '&' and must only record the error. Emitting a Null token may interfere with later stages or output logic.]

122.reason: [When encountering a single '&' or '|', the lexer correctly records error 'a', but it should NOT generate any token (especially not a dummy "HELL" token). According to the problem, for erroneous input, only error.txt should be produced; lexer.txt should not contain tokens for invalid symbols. The insertion of a Null-type token here interferes with correct output behavior and may cause incorrect token emission even in error cases.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: emitting a dummy token for invalid single '|' is wrong. Only the error should be recorded, no token should be generated.]
